title: commiphora mollis
intro: Commiphora mollis, also known as East African myrrh or Swellings tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its thorny branches and aromatic resin. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, inflammation, and respiratory issues, thanks to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Commiphora mollis include reducing pain, fighting off infections, and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine, skincare products, and aromatherapy.
